notes.note: Cicero's me hercule --- a colloquial Roman asseveration, lit. 'so help me Hercules.' Preserved here in its Roman form rather than flattened to 'truly' or 'indeed.'

notes.anchor: posted by you yourself to the ends of the earth
notes.note: M. Marcellus, as consul of 51 BC, presided over the senatorial allocation of provinces under the lex Pompeia of 52, by which a five-year gap was required between magistracy and provincial command. Cicero, having declined the provinces in 63 and 62, was sent out under this rule.

notes.anchor: what you, with the senate's resolution and by law, have set as my term
notes.note: The lex Pompeia and the consequent senatus consultum fixed the proconsular term at one year. Cicero is anxious already, on the road, that nothing be added to it; the same fear runs through the rest of the Cilician correspondence.

After Carrhae in 53 BC, Parthian raids across the Euphrates into Roman Syria and the kingdoms east of Cilicia were a recurring threat. The discreet line here --- Cicero will not write to a consul about Parthian news, lest the letter be read as official --- is characteristic of the proconsular reserve.
